questions.questionText: In a hydrogen like atom, when an electron jumps from the M-shell to the L-shell, the wavelength of emitted radiation is $$\lambda $$. If an electron jumps from N-shell to the L-shell, the wavelength of emitted radiation will be:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
For M $$ \to $$ L steel

$${1 \over \lambda }$$ = K $$\left( {{1 \over {{2^2}}} - {1 \over {{3^2}}}} \right) = {{K \times 5} \over {36}}$$

for N $$ \to $$ L

$${1 \over {\lambda '}}$$ = K$$\left( {{1 \over {{2^2}}} - {1 \over {{4^2}}}} \right) = {{K \times 3} \over {16}}$$

$$\lambda ' = {{20} \over {27}}\lambda $$

questions.questionText: Seven capacitors, each of capacitance 2 $$\mu $$F, are to be connected in a configuration to obtain an effective capacitance of $$\left( {{6 \over {13}}} \right)\mu F.$$ Which of the combinations, shown in figures below, will achieve the desired value
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Ceq = $${6 \over {13}}$$$$\mu $$F

Therefore three capacitors most be in parallel to get 6 in

$${1 \over {{C_{eq}}}} = {1 \over {3C}} + {1 \over C} + {1 \over C} + {1 \over C} + {1 \over C}$$

Ceq = $${{3C} \over {13}}$$ = $${6 \over {13}}$$$$\mu $$F

questions.questionText: A particle of mass m is moving in a straight line with momentum p. Starting at time t = 0, a force F = kt acts in the same direction on the moving particle during time interval T so that its momentum changes from p to 3p. Here k is a constant. The value of T is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${{dp} \over {dt}} = F = kt$$

$$\int_P^{3P} {dP} = \int_0^T {kt\,dt} $$

$$2p = {{K{T^2}} \over 2}$$

$$T = 2\sqrt {{P \over K}} $$

questions.questionText:
An amplitude modulated signal is plotted below :



Which one of the following best describes the above signal ?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Analysis of graph says

(1)   Amplitude varies as 8 $$-$$ 10 V or 9 $$ \pm $$ 1

(2)  Two time period as

100 $$\mu $$s (signal wave) & 8 $$\mu $$s (carrier wave)

Hence signal is $$\left[ {9 \pm 1\sin \left( {{{2\pi t} \over {{T_1}}}} \right)} \right]\sin \left( {{{2\pi t} \over {{T_2}}}} \right)$$

= 9 $$ \pm $$ 1sin (2$$\pi $$ $$ \times $$ 104t) sin 2.5$$\pi $$ $$ \times $$ 105 t

questions.questionText: In the experimental set up of metre bridge shown in the figure, the null point is obtained at a distance of 40 cm from A. If a 10 $$\Omega $$ resistor is connected in series with R1, the null point shifts by 10 cm. The resistance that should be connected in parallel with (R1 + 10) $$\Omega $$ such that the null point shifts back to its initial position is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${{{R_1}} \over {{R_2}}} = {2 \over 3}\,\,$$                                      . . .(i)

$${{{R_1} + 10} \over {{R_2}}} = 1 \Rightarrow {R_1} + 10 = {R_2}$$     . . .(ii)

$${{2{R_2}} \over 3} + 10 = {R_2}$$

$$10 = {{{R_2}} \over 3} \Rightarrow {R_2} = 30\Omega $$

& $${R_1} = 20\Omega $$

$${{{{30 \times R} \over {30 + R}}} \over {30}} = {2 \over 3}$$

$$R = 60\,\Omega $$

questions.questionText: In the circuit shown, the potential difference between A and B is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Potential difference across AB will be equal to battery equivalent across CD

VAB $$=$$ VCD $$=$$ $${{{{{E_1}} \over {{r_1}}} + {{{E_2}} \over {{r_2}}} + {{{E_3}} \over {{r_3}}}} \over {{1 \over {{r_1}}} + {1 \over {{r_2}}} + {1 \over {{r_3}}}}} = {{{1 \over 1} + {2 \over 1} + {3 \over 1}} \over {{1 \over 1} + {1 \over 1} + {1 \over 1}}}$$

$$=$$ $${6 \over 3}$$ $$=$$ 2V

questions.questionText: A galvanometer having a resistance of 20 $$\Omega $$ and 30 divisions on both sides has figure of merit 0.005 ampere/division. The resistance that should be connected in series such that it can be used as a voltmeter upto 15 volt, is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Rg = 20$$\Omega $$

NL = NR = N = 30

FOM = $${1 \over \phi }$$ = 0.005 A/Div.

Current sentivity = CS = $$\left( {{1 \over {0.005}}} \right)$$ = $${\phi \over {\rm I}}$$

$${\rm I}$$gmax = 0.005 $$ \times $$ 30

= 15 $$ \times $$ 10$$-$$2 = 0.15

15 = 0.15 [20 + R]

100 = 20 + R

R = 80

questions.questionText: In a photoelectric experiment, the wavelength of the light incident on a metal is changed from 300 nm to 400 nm. The decrease in the stopping potential is close to:  ($${{{hc} \over e}}$$ = 1240 nm eV)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${{hc} \over {{\lambda _1}}} = \phi + e$$V1        . . . (i)

$${{hc} \over {{\lambda _2}}} = \phi + e$$V2        . . . (ii)

(i) $$-$$ (ii)

hc$$\left( {{1 \over {{\lambda _1}}} - {1 \over {{\lambda _2}}}} \right)$$ = e(V1 $$-$$ V2)

$$ \Rightarrow $$  V1 $$-$$ V2 = $${{hc} \over e}$$$$\left( {{{{\lambda _2} - {\lambda _1}} \over {{\lambda _1} - {\lambda _2}}}} \right)$$

= (1240nm $$-$$ V) $${{100nm} \over {300nm \times 400nm}}$$

= 1V

questions.questionText:
A copper wire is wound on a wooden frame, whose shape is that of an equilateral triangle. If the linear
dimension of each side of the frame is increased by a factor of 3, keeping the number of turns of the coil per
unit length of the frame the same, then the self inductance of the coil:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Total length L will remain constant

L = (3a) N        (N = total turns)

and length of winding = (d) N

                         (d = diameter of wire)


self inductance = $$\mu $$0n2A$$\ell $$

= $$\mu $$0n2$$\left( {{{\sqrt 3 {a^2}} \over 4}} \right)$$ dN

$$ \propto $$ a2 N $$ \propto $$ a

So self inductance will become 3 times

questions.questionText:
A 27 mW laser beam has a cross-sectional area of 10 mm2. The magnitude of the maximum electric field in this electromagnetic wave is given by :
[Given permittivity of space $$ \in $$0 = 9 $$ \times $$ 10–12 SI units, Speed of light c = 3 $$ \times $$ 108 m/s]
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Intensity of EM wave is given by

$${\rm I} = {{Power} \over {Area}} = {1 \over 2}{\varepsilon _0}E_0^2C$$

$$ = {{27 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} \over {10 \times {{10}^{ - 6}}}}$$ $$ = {1 \over 2} \times 9 \times {10^{ - 12}} \times {E^2} \times 3 \times {10^8}$$

$$E = \sqrt 2 \times {10^3}kv/m$$

$$ = 1.4$$ kv/m

questions.questionText: The circuit shown below contains two ideal diodes, each with a forward resistance of 50 $$\Omega $$. If the battery voltage is 6 V, the current through the 100 $$\Omega $$ resistance (in Amperes) is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution: I = $${6 \over {300}}$$ = 0.02 (D<sub>2</sub> is in reverse bias)

questions.questionText: An electric field of 1000 V/m is applied to an electric dipole at angle of 45<sup>o</sup>. The value of electric dipole moment is 10<sup>–29</sup> C.m. What is the potential energy of the electric dipole?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
U = $$-$$ $$\overrightarrow P .\overrightarrow E $$

= $$-$$ PE cos $$\theta $$

= $$-$$ (10$$-$$29) (103) cos 45o

= $$-$$ 0.707 $$ \times $$ 10$$-$$26 J

= $$-$$ 7 $$ \times $$ 10$$-$$27 J.

questions.questionText: A monochromatic light is incident at a certain angle on an equilateral triangular prism and suffers minimum deviation. If the refractive index of the material of the prism is $$\sqrt 3 $$, then the angle of incidence is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
i = e

r1 = r2 = $${A \over 2}$$ = 30o

by Snell's law

1 $$ \times $$ sin i = $$\sqrt 3 \times {1 \over 2} = {{\sqrt 3 } \over 2}$$

i = 60

questions.questionText: A thermometer graduated according to a linear scale reads a value x<sub>0</sub> when in contact with boiling water, and x<sub>0</sub>/3 when in contact with ice. What is the temperature of an object in <sup>o</sup>C, if this thermometer in the contact with the object reads x<sub>0</sub>/2 ?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$ \Rightarrow $$   ToC = $${{{x_0}} \over 6}$$ & $$\left( {{x_0} - {{{x_0}} \over 3}} \right)$$ = (100 $$-$$ 0oC)

x0 = $${{300} \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  ToC = $${{150} \over 6}$$ = 25oC

questions.questionText: When 100 g of a liquid A at 100<sup>o</sup>C is added to 50 g of a liquid B at temperature 75<sup>o</sup>C, the temperature of the mixture becomes 90<sup>o</sup>C. The temperature of the mixture, if 100 g of liquid A at 100<sup>o</sup>C is added to 50 g of liquid B at 50<sup>o</sup>C, will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
In a process, temperature and volume of one mole of an ideal monoatomic gas are varied according to the relation VT = K, where K is a constant. In this process the temperature of the gas is increased by $$\Delta $$T. The
amount of heat absorbed by gas is (R is gas constant) :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
VT = K

$$ \Rightarrow $$  V$$\left( {{{PV} \over {nR}}} \right)$$ = k $$ \Rightarrow $$ PV2 = K

$$ \because $$  C = $${R \over {1 - x}} + $$ Cv (For polytropic process)

C = $${R \over {1 - 2}} + {{3R} \over 2}$$ = $${R \over 2}$$

$$ \therefore $$  $$\Delta $$Q = nC $$\Delta $$T

= $${R \over 2} \times \Delta $$T

questions.questionText: A metal ball of mass 0.1 kg is heated upto 500<sup>o</sup>C and dropped into a vessel of heat capacity 800 JK<sup>–1</sup> and containing 0.5 kg water. The initial temperature of water and vessel is 30<sup>o</sup>C. What is the approximate percentage increment in the temperature of the water? [Specific Heat Capacities of water and metal are, respectively, 4200 Jkg<sup>–1</sup> and 400 Jkg<sup>–1</sup> K<sup>–1</sup>
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two rods A and B of identical dimensions are at temperature 30<sup>°</sup>C. If A is heated upto 180<sup>o</sup>C and B upto T<sup>o</sup>C, then the new lengths are the same. If the ratio of the coefficients of linear expansion of A and B is 4 : 3, then the value of T is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$\Delta {\ell _1} = \Delta {\ell _2}$$

$$\ell {\alpha _1}\Delta {T_1} = \ell {\alpha _2}\Delta {T_2}$$

$${{{\alpha _1}} \over {{\alpha _2}}} = {{\Delta {T_1}} \over {\Delta {T_2}}}$$

$${4 \over 3} = {{T - 30} \over {180 - 30}}$$

$$T = {230^o}C$$

questions.questionText: A paramagnetic substance in the form of a cube with sides 1 cm has a magnetic dipole moment of 20 $$ \times $$ 10<sup>–6</sup> J/ T when a magnetic intensity of 60 $$ \times $$ 10<sup>3</sup> A/m is applied. Its magnetic susceptibility is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A particle of mass m and charge q is in an electric and magnetic field given by
$$\overrightarrow E = 2\widehat i + 3\widehat j;\,\,\,\overrightarrow B = 4\widehat j + 6\widehat k.$$

The charged particle is shifted from he origin to the point P(x = 1; y = 1) along a straight path. The magnitude of the total work done is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${\overrightarrow F _{net}} = q\overrightarrow E + q\left( {\overrightarrow v \times \overrightarrow B } \right)$$

$$ = \left( {2q\widehat i + 3q\widehat j} \right) + q\left( {\overrightarrow v \times \overrightarrow B } \right)$$

$$W = {\overrightarrow F _{net}}.\overrightarrow S $$

$$=$$ 2q + 3q

$$=$$ 5q

questions.questionText: The major product of the following reaction is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: First Markonikov’s addition on alkene followed by intramolecular Friedel-Craft alkylation takes place.

questions.questionText: Which of the following compounds reacts with ethylmagnesium bromide and also decolourizes bromine water solution:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
For option (B) :

C2H5MgBr performs addition reaction with - C $$ \equiv $$ N.
C2H5MgBr performs substitution reaction with ester -COOCH3.

Conjugated dienes (two double bonds separated by a single bond) gives Br water test. Either 1-2 or 1-4 addition can happen.

For option (C) :

C2H5MgBr reacts with -OH group and creates alkane C2H6.

As here C = C present, so this compound gives Br water tests.

questions.questionText: The major product obtained in the following reaction is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: In LiAlH4, H- is the attacking species which is a strong nucleophile and performs both nucleophilc substitution and nucleophilic addition reaction. In C = C, pi bond is created by pure p orbital with side by side overlapping which create $$\pi $$ cloud and $$\pi $$ cloud creates negative charge around it. As H- is also negative charged so LiAlH4 can't reduce C = C.

questions.questionText: The major product obtained in the following conversion is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
In the following compound,



the favourable site/s for protonation is/are :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
At (a) position lone pair of N atom is in resonance with $$\pi $$ bond so lone pair can't donate easily to H+ ion.

At (b) position N atom is directly connected with double bond so lone pair of N atom don't perticipate in resonance so lone pair can be donated easily to H+ ion.

At (c) position N atom is directly connected with double bond so lone pair of N atom don't perticipate in resonance so lone pair can be donated easily to H+ ion.

At (d) position N atom is directly connected with double bond so lone pair of N atom don't perticipate in resonance so lone pair can be donated easily to H+ ion.

At (e) position lone pair of N atom is in resonance with $$\pi $$ bond so lone pair can't donate easily to H+ ion.

$$ \therefore $$ (b), (c) and (d) are the favourable sites for protonation.

questions.questionText:
The correct match between Item I and Item II is :

Item I Item II
(A) Ester test (P) Tyr
(B) Carbylamine test (Q) Asp
(C) Phthalein dye test
 (R) Ser
(S) Lys
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The reaction 2X $$ \to $$ B is a zeroth order reaction. If the initial concentration of X is 0.2 M, the half-life is 6 h. When the initial concentration of X is 0.5 M, the time required to reach its final concentration of 0.2 M will
be:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
For zero order reaction,

t1/2 = $${{{a_0}} \over {2k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ k = $${{{a_0}} \over {2{t_{1/2}}}}$$ = $${{0.2} \over {2 \times 6}}$$ = 1.67 $$ \times $$ 10-2 mol L–1h–1

For zero order reaction,

A0 - At = kt

$$ \Rightarrow $$ 0.5 - 0.2 = 1.67 $$ \times $$ 10-2 t

$$ \Rightarrow $$ t = $${{0.3} \over {1.67 \times {{10}^{ - 2}}}}$$ = 18 h

questions.questionText:
The correct match between Item I and Item II is :

Item I Item II
(A) Allosteric effect (P) Molecule binding to the active site of enzyme
(B) Competitive inhibitor (Q) Molecule crucial for communication in the body
(C) Receptor
 (R) Molecule binding to a site other than the active site of
enzyme
(D) Poison (S) Molecule binding to the enzyme covalently
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Allosteric effect $$ \to $$ Molecule binding to a site other than the active site of enzyme

Competitive inhibitor $$ \to $$ Molecule binding to the active site of enzyme

Receptor $$ \to $$ Molecule crucial for communication in the body

Poison $$ \to $$ Molecule binding to the enzyme covalently

questions.questionText: A compound 'X' on treatment with Br<sub>2</sub>/NaOH, provided C<sub>3</sub>H<sub>9</sub>N, which gives positive carbylamine test. Compound 'X' is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Br2/NaOH(Hoffman Hypobromide reagent) converts amide into primary amine having one carbon atom less, which gives carbylamine test. In the Amide there should be
-CONH2 group to produce primary amine.

questions.questionText:
The coordination number of Th in K4[Th(C2O4)4(OH2)2] is :
(C2O$${_4^{2 - }}$$ = Oxalato)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Oxalato (C2O42–) is a bidentate and H2O is unidentate ligand.

4C2O42– creates 8 covalent bonds.

2H2O creates 2 covalent bonds.

$$ \therefore $$ Around Th 10 coordinate covalent bonds will be present.

questions.questionText: The number of bridging CO ligand(s) and Co-Co bond(s) in Co<sub>2</sub>(CO)<sub>8</sub>, respectively are :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$$\underline A \,\,\buildrel {4KOH,{O_2}} \over \longrightarrow \,\,\mathop {2\underline B }\limits_{\left( {Green} \right)} \,\, + \,\,2{H_2}O$$

$$3\underline B \,\,\buildrel {4HCl} \over \longrightarrow \,\,\mathop {2\underline C }\limits_{\left( {Purple} \right)} \,\, + \,\,Mn{O_2} + 2{H_2}O$$

$$2\underline C \,\,\buildrel {{H_2}O.KI} \over \longrightarrow \,\,\mathop {2\underline A }\limits_{\left( {Purple} \right)} \,\, + \,\,2KOH\,\, + \,\,\underline D $$

In the above sequence of reactions, $${\underline A }$$ and $${\underline D }$$, respectively, are :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
$$Mn{O_2}(A) \,\,\buildrel {4KOH,{O_2}} \over \longrightarrow \,\,\mathop {2K_2MnO_4(B) }\limits_{\left( {Green} \right)} \,\, + \,\,2{H_2}O$$

$$3K_2MnO_4(B) \,\,\buildrel {4HCl} \over \longrightarrow \,\,\mathop {2K_2MnO_4(C) }\limits_{\left( {Purple} \right)} \,\, + \,\,Mn{O_2} + 2{H_2}O$$

$$2K_2MnO_4(C) \,\,\buildrel {{H_2}O.KI} \over \longrightarrow \,\,\mathop {2 Mn{O_2}(A) }\limits_{\left( {Purple} \right)} \,\, + \,\,2KOH\,\, + \,\,KIO_3(D) $$

$$ \therefore $$ A $$ \to $$ MnO2

D $$ \to $$ KIO3

questions.questionText:
Given the equilibrium constant:

KC of the reaction :

Cu(s) + 2Ag+ (aq) $$ \to $$ Cu2+ (aq) + 2Ag(s) is

10 $$ \times $$ 1015, calculate the E$$_{cell}^0$$ of this reaciton at 298 K

[2.303 $${{RT} \over F}$$ at 298 K = 0.059V]
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
We know,

$$\Delta $$Go = -RTln(KC) ....(1)

Also $$\Delta $$Go = -nF$$E_{cell}^o$$ ....(2)

$$ \therefore $$ -nF$$E_{cell}^o$$ = -RTln(KC)

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$E_{cell}^o$$ = $${{RT} \over {nF}}\ln \left( {{K_C}} \right)$$

= $$2.303{{RT} \over {nF}}\log \left( {{K_C}} \right)$$

= $${{0.059} \over 2}\log \left( {10 \times {{10}^{15}}} \right)$$

( n = no of electron transferred = 2 )

= 0.059 $$ \times $$ $${{16} \over 2}$$

= 0.059 $$ \times $$ 8

= 0.472 V

questions.questionText: Taj Mahal is being slowly disfigured and discoloured. This is primarily due to :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Taj mahal is slowely disfigured and discoloured due to acid rain.

The primary acid component of acid rain is sulfuric acid(H2SO4) which reacts with Marble(CaCO3) and gives aqueous ion. This aqueous ions get washed away in the water flow and marble get disfigured and discoloured.

CaCO3(s) + H2SO4 $$ \to $$

Ca2+(aq) + SO42-(aq) + H2O + CO2

questions.questionText: The higher concentration of which gas in air can cause stiffness of flower buds?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Due to acid rain in plants high concentration of SO<sub>2</sub> makes the flower buds stiff and makes them
fall.

questions.questionText: Which of the following compounds will produce a precipitate with AgNO<sub>3</sub> ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The relative stability of +1 oxidation state of group 13 elements follows the order :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Inert pair effect gradually increases down the group. Hence, stability of lower oxidation state increases down the group.

$$ \therefore $$ AI < Ga < In < TI

questions.questionText: The hydride that is NOT electron deficient :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: SiH<sub>4</sub> has complete octet hence it is not an electron deficient hydride.

questions.questionText: The correct option with respect to the Pauling electronegativity values of the element is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Electronegativity increases from left to right in a period and decreases down the group. The correct orders are

Si > Al, Ga < Ge, Te < Se and P < S.

questions.questionText: The homopolymer formed from 4-hydroxy-butanoic acid is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Two lines $${{x - 3} \over 1} = {{y + 1} \over 3} = {{z - 6} \over { - 1}}$$ and $${{x + 5} \over 7} = {{y - 2} \over { - 6}} = {{z - 3} \over 4}$$  intersect at the point R. The reflection of R in the xy-plane has coordinates :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Point on L1 ($$\lambda $$ + 3, 3$$\lambda $$ $$-$$ 1, $$-$$$$\lambda $$ + 6)

Point on L2 (7$$\mu $$ $$-$$ 5, $$-$$6$$\mu $$ + 2, 4$$\mu $$ + 3

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $$\lambda $$ + 3 = 7$$\mu $$ $$-$$ 5      . . . . (i)

3$$\lambda $$ $$-$$ 1 = $$-$$6$$\mu $$ + 2       . . . .(ii)

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $$\lambda $$ = $$-$$1, $$\mu $$ = 1

point R(2, $$-$$ 4, 7)

Reflection is (2, $$-$$4, $$-$$ 7)

questions.questionText: If the point (2, $$\alpha $$, $$\beta $$) lies on the plane which passes through the points (3, 4, 2) and (7, 0, 6) and is perpendicular to the plane 2x – 5y = 15, then 2$$\alpha $$ – 3$$\beta $$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Normal vector of plane

$$ = \left| {\matrix{ {\widehat i} & {\widehat j} & {\widehat k} \cr 2 & { - 5} & 0 \cr 4 & { - 4} & 5 \cr } } \right|$$

$$ = - 4\left( {5\widehat i + 2\widehat j - 3\widehat k} \right)$$

equation of plane is

5(x $$-$$ 7) + 2y $$-$$ 3(z $$-$$ 6) = 0

5x + 2y $$-$$ 3z = 17

5 $$ \times $$ 2 + 2$$\alpha $$ $$-$$ 3$$\beta $$ = 17

$$ \therefore $$  2$$\alpha $$ $$-$$ 3$$\beta $$ = 17 $$-$$ 10 = 7

questions.questionText: Let f(x) = $${x \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {x^2}} }} - {{d - x} \over {\sqrt {{b^2} + {{\left( {d - x} \right)}^2}} }},\,\,$$ x $$\, \in $$ R, where a, b and d are non-zero real constants. Then :
options: f is an increasing function of x
f is neither increasing nor decreasing function of x
f ' is not a continuous function of x
f is a decreasing function of x
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$f\left( x \right) = {x \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {x^2}} }} - {{d - x} \over {\sqrt {{b^2} + {{\left( {d - x} \right)}^2}} }}$$

$$f'\left( x \right) = {{{a^2}} \over {{{\left( {{a^2} + {x^2}} \right)}^{3/2}}}} + {{{b^2}} \over {{{\left( {{b^2} + {{\left( {d - x} \right)}^2}} \right)}^{3/2}}}} > 0\forall x \in R$$

f(x) is an increasing function.

questions.questionText: The area (in sq. units) in the first quadrant bounded by the parabola, y = x<sup>2</sup> + 1, the tangent to it at the point (2, 5) and the coordinate axes is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution: Area $$ = \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)dx - {1 \over 2}} \left( {{5 \over 4}} \right)\left( 5 \right) = {{37} \over {24}}$$

questions.questionText: Let (x + 10)<sup>50</sup> + (x $$-$$ 10)<sup>50</sup> = a<sub>0</sub> + a<sub>1</sub>x + a<sub>2</sub>x<sup>2</sup> + . . . . + a<sub>50</sub>x<sup>50</sup>, for all x $$ \in $$ R; then $${{{a_2}} \over {{a_0}}}$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let S<sub>n</sub> = 1 + q + q<sup>2</sup> + . . . . . + q<sup>n</sup>  and  T<sub>n</sub> = 1 + $$\left( {{{q + 1} \over 2}} \right) + {\left( {{{q + 1} \over 2}} \right)^2}$$ + . . . . . .+  $${\left( {{{q + 1} \over 2}} \right)^n}$$  where q is a real number and   q $$ \ne $$ 1. If   <sup>101</sup>C<sub>1</sub> + <sup>101</sup>C<sub>2</sub> . S<sub>1</sub> + .... + <sup>101</sup>C<sub>101</sub> . S<sub>100</sub> = $$\alpha $$T<sub>100</sub> then $$\alpha $$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
101C1 + 101C2S1 + . . . . . . . + 101C101S100

$$=$$ $$\alpha $$T100

101C1 + 101C2(1 + q) + 101C3(1 + q + q2) +

         . . . . . .+101C101(1 + q + . . . . . + q100)

$$ = 2\alpha {{\left( {1 - {{\left( {{{1 + q} \over 2}} \right)}^{101}}} \right)} \over {\left( {1 - q} \right)}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  101C1(1 $$-$$ q) + 101C2(1 $$-$$ q2) +

         . . . . . . + 101C101(1 $$-$$ q101)

$$ = 2\alpha \left( {1 - {{\left( {{{1 + q} \over 2}} \right)}^{101}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  (2101 $$-$$ 1) $$-$$ ((1 + q)101 $$-$$ 1)

$$ = 2\alpha \left( {1 - {{\left( {{{1 + q} \over 2}} \right)}^{101}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${2^{101}}\left( {1 - {{\left( {{{1 + q} \over 2}} \right)}^{101}}} \right) = 2\alpha \left( {1 - {{\left( {{{1 + q} \over 2}} \right)}^{101}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $$\alpha = {2^{100}}$$

questions.questionText: Let z be a complex number such that |z| + z = 3 + i (where i = $$\sqrt { - 1} $$).  Then |z| is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$\left| z \right| + z = 3 + i$$

$$z = 3 - \left| z \right| + i$$

Let  $$3 - \left| z \right| = a \Rightarrow \left| z \right| = \left( {3 - a} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow z = a + i \Rightarrow \left| z \right| = \sqrt {{a^2} + 1} $$

$$ \Rightarrow 9 + {a^2} - 6a = {a^2} + 1 \Rightarrow a = {8 \over 6} = {4 \over 3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| z \right| = 3 - {4 \over 3} = {5 \over 3}$$

questions.questionText: The integral  $$\int\limits_{\pi /6}^{\pi /4} {{{dx} \over {\sin 2x\left( {{{\tan }^5}x + {{\cot }^5}x} \right)}}} $$  equals :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
I $$=$$ $$\int\limits_{\pi /6}^{\pi /4} {{{dx} \over {\sin 2x\left( {{{\tan }^5}x + {{\cot }^5}x} \right)}}} $$

$${\rm I} = {1 \over 2}\int\limits_{\pi /6}^{\pi /4} {{{{{\tan }^4}x{{\sec }^2}xdx} \over {\left( {1 + {{\tan }^{10}}x} \right)}}} $$   Put tan5x $$=$$ t

$${\rm I} = {1 \over {10}}\int\limits_{{{\left( {{1 \over {\sqrt 3 }}} \right)}^5}}^1 {{{dt} \over {1 + {t^2}}}} = {1 \over {10}}\left( {{\pi \over 4} - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}{1 \over {9\sqrt 3 }}} \right)$$

questions.questionText:
The solution of the differential equation,

$${{dy} \over {dx}}$$ = (x – y)2, when y(1) = 1, is :
options: $$-$$ log<sub>e</sub> $$\left| {{{1 + x - y} \over {1 - x + y}}} \right|$$ = x + y $$-$$ 2
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
x $$-$$ y = t

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $${{dy} \over {dx}} = 1 - {{dt} \over {dx}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  1 $$-$$ $${{dt} \over {dx}}$$ = t2 $$ \Rightarrow $$  $$\int {{{dt} \over {1 - {t^2}}}} $$ = $$\int {1dx} $$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${1 \over 2}\ell n\left( {{{1 + t} \over {1 - t}}} \right) = x + \lambda $$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${1 \over 2}\ell n\left( {{{1 + x - y} \over {1 - x + y}}} \right) = x + \lambda $$ given y(1) = 1

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${1 \over 2}\ell n(1) = 1 + \lambda \Rightarrow \lambda = - 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $$\ell n\left( {{{1 + x - y} \over {1 - x + y}}} \right)$$ = 2(x $$-$$ 1)

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $$ - \ell n\left( {{{1 - x + y} \over {1 + x - y}}} \right)$$ = 2(x $$-$$ 1)

questions.questionText: Let the length of the latus rectum of an ellipse with its major axis along x-axis and centre at the origin, be 8. If the distance between the foci of this ellipse is equal to the length of its minor axis, then which one of the following points lies on it?
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$${{2{b^2}} \over a} = 8$$ and 2ae $$=$$ 2b

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${b \over a}$$ = e and 1 $$-$$ e2 = e2 $$ \Rightarrow $$ e $$=$$ $${1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  b = 4$$\sqrt 2 $$ and a $$=$$ 8

So equation of ellipse is $${{{x^2}} \over {64}} + {{{y^2}} \over {32}} = 1$$

questions.questionText:
The number of functions f from {1, 2, 3, ...., 20} onto {1, 2, 3, ...., 20} such that f(k) is a multiple of 3,
whenever k is a multiple of 4, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Given that $f(k)$ is a multiple of 3 whenever $k$ is a multiple of 4, we need to consider how to map elements from the domain {1, 2, 3, ..., 20} to the codomain {1, 2, 3, ..., 20} following this rule.

1. We first consider the subset of the domain that consists of multiples of 4: {4, 8, 12, 16, 20}. There are 5 elements in this subset.

2. We then consider the subset of the codomain that consists of multiples of 3: {3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18}. There are 6 elements in this subset.

3. According to the given condition, each of the 5 multiples of 4 must be mapped to a multiple of 3. This can be done in ${ }^6C_5 \cdot 5! = 6!$ ways, considering that there are 6 options for each of the 5 multiples of 4 (each choice constitutes a combination), and we then consider the permutations of these 5 choices.

4. The remaining 15 elements in the domain (20 original elements minus the 5 multiples of 4) can be mapped onto the remaining 15 elements in the codomain (20 original elements minus the 6 multiples of 3, plus one multiple of 3 that has been assigned to a multiple of 4). This can be done in $15!$ ways.

So, combining these two cases, the total number of onto functions $f$ is $6! \times 15!$, which corresponds to option C.

questions.questionText: Let a function f : (0, $$\infty $$) $$ \to $$ (0, $$\infty $$) be defined by f(x) = $$\left| {1 - {1 \over x}} \right|$$. Then f is :
options: not injective but it is surjective
both injective as well as surjective
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$f\left( x \right) = \left| {1 - {1 \over x}} \right| = {{\left| {x - 1} \right|} \over x} = \left\{ {\matrix{ {{{1 - x} \over x}} & {0 < x \le 1} \cr {{{x - 1} \over x}} & {x \ge 1} \cr } } \right.$$


$$ \Rightarrow $$  f(x) is not injective

but range of function is $$\left[ {0,\infty } \right)$$

Remarks :  If co-domain is $$\left[ {0,\infty } \right)$$, then f(x) will be surjective.

questions.questionText: A circle cuts a chord of length 4a on the x-axis and passes through a point on the y-axis, distant 2b from the origin. Then the locus of the centre of this circle, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let equation of circle is

x2 + y2 + 2fx + 2fy + e = 0, it passes through (0, 2b)

$$ \Rightarrow $$  0 + 4b2 + 2g $$ \times $$ 0 + 4f + c = 0

$$ \Rightarrow $$  4b2 + 4f + c = 0       . . . (i)

$$2\sqrt {{g^2} - c} = 4a$$      . . . (ii)

g2 $$-$$ c = 4a2 $$ \Rightarrow $$ c = $$\left( {{g^2} - 4{a^2}} \right)$$

Putting in equation (1)

$$ \Rightarrow $$  4b2 + 4f + g2 $$-$$ 4a2 = 0

$$ \Rightarrow $$  x2 + 4y + 4(b2 $$-$$ a2) = 0, it represent a hyperbola.

questions.questionText:
If a hyperbola has length of its conjugate axis equal to 5 and the distance between its foci is 13, then the
eccentricity of the hyperbola is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
2b = 5 and 2ae = 13

b2 = a2(e2 $$-$$ 1) $$ \Rightarrow $$  $${{25} \over 4}$$ = $${{169} \over 4}$$ $$-$$ a2

$$ \Rightarrow $$  a $$=$$ 6 $$ \Rightarrow $$  e $$=$$ $${{13} \over {12}}$$

questions.questionText: If   $$\int {{{x + 1} \over {\sqrt {2x - 1} }}} \,dx$$ = f(x) $$\sqrt {2x - 1} $$ + C, where C is a constant of integration, then f(x) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$\sqrt {2x - 1} = t \Rightarrow 2x - 1 = {t^2} \Rightarrow 2dx = 2t.dt$$

$$\int {{{x + 1} \over {\sqrt {2x - 1} }}dx = \int {{{{{{t^2} + 1} \over 2} + 1} \over t}tdt = \int {{{{t^2} + 3} \over 2}dt} } } $$

$$ = {1 \over 2}\left( {{{{t^3}} \over 3} + 3t} \right) = {t \over 6}\left( {{t^2} + 9} \right) + c$$

$$ = \sqrt {2x - 1} \left( {{{2x - 1 + 9} \over 6}} \right) + c = \sqrt {2x - 1} \left( {{{x + 4} \over 3}} \right) + c$$

$$ \Rightarrow f\left( x \right) = {{x + 4} \over 3}$$

questions.questionText:
All x satisfying the inequality (cot<sup>–1</sup>
x)<sup>2</sup>– 7(cot<sup>–1</sup> x) + 10 > 0, lie in the interval :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
cot$$-$$1 x > 5,    cot$$-$$1 x < 2

$$ \Rightarrow $$  x < cot5,   x > cot2

questions.questionText: $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{x\cot \left( {4x} \right)} \over {{{\sin }^2}x{{\cot }^2}\left( {2x} \right)}}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution: $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{x{{\tan }^2}2x} \over {\tan 4x{{\sin }^2}x}} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{x\left( {{{{{\tan }^2}2x} \over {4{x^2}}}} \right)4{x^2}} \over {\left( {{{\tan 4x} \over {4x}}} \right)4x\left( {{{{{\sin }^2}x} \over {{x^2}}}} \right){x^2}}} = 1$$

questions.questionText: Let K be the set of all real values of x where the function f(x) = sin |x| – |x| + 2(x – $$\pi $$) cos |x| is not differentiable. Then the set K is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
f(x) = sin$$\left| x \right| - \left| x \right|$$ + 2(x $$-$$ $$\pi $$) cosx

$$ \because $$  sin$$\left| x \right|$$ $$-$$ $$\left| x \right|$$ is differentiable function at c = 0

$$ \therefore $$  k = $$\phi $$

questions.questionText: Contrapositive of the statement " If two numbers are not equal, then their squares are not equal." is :
options: If the squares of two numbers are equal, then the numbers are not equal
If the squares of two numbers are equal, then the numbers are equal
If the squares of two numbers are not equal, then the numbers are equal
If the squares of two numbers are not equal, then the numbers are not equal
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let,

p : two numbers are not equal

q : squares of two numbers are not equal

Contrapositive of p $$ \to $$ q is $$ \sim $$q $$ \to $$ $$ \sim $$p.

$$ \therefore $$ $$ \sim $$q $$ \to $$ $$ \sim $$p means "If the squares of two numbers are equal, then the numbers are equal".

questions.questionText:
Let A and B be two invertible matrices of order 3 $$ \times $$ 3. If det(ABAT) = 8 and det(AB–1) = 8,
then det (BA–1 BT) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$${\left| A \right|^2}.\left| B \right| = 8$$

and $${{\left| A \right|} \over {\left| B \right|}} = 8 \Rightarrow \left| A \right| = 4$$

and $$\left| B \right| = {1 \over 2}$$

$$ \therefore $$  det(BA$$-$$1. BT) $$ = {1 \over 4} \times {1 \over 4} = {1 \over {16}}$$
